content:
# Tiered Knowledge System — Complete Domain Map (Research Synthesis)

## Domain 1: Memory Type Classification (DECIDED)
5 types: Structural, Decisional, Episodic, Analytical, Procedural
Each type has a distinct decay profile (alpha values for power-law decay).

## Domain 2: Tier Lifecycle (DECIDED)
4 tiers: Hot (HNSW indexed) → Warm (HNSW indexed, deprioritized) → Cold (not in HNSW, embedding preserved, reversible) → Forgotten (compacted into summary)
Cold is reversible — entries auto-promote on access.

## Domain 3: Access Tracking
- Two tiers of access: Tier 1 (returned to agent) and Tier 2 (candidate but filtered)
- Entry-level fields: last_accessed, access_count, access_agents (u8), decay_score (f32)
- In-memory DashMap buffer with periodic Redb flush (every 60s or 256 entries)
- Agent breadth > raw count: 5 different agents accessing > 5 accesses by same agent
- EMA-based decay scoring with configurable half-life

## Domain 4: Decay Mathematics
- Power-law R = (1+t)^(-alpha) preferred over exponential (fat tail preserves cold entries)
- Alpha values: Structural=0, Procedural=0.10, Decisional=0.15, Analytical=0.30, Episodic=0.60
- Composite score: recency + importance + access_frequency + graph_connectivity
- Confidence modulates decay: effective_age = actual_age / confidence
- Thresholds with hysteresis: Hot>=0.50, Warm>=0.25, Cold>=0.05, promotion gap=0.10

## Domain 5: Forgetting Policies
- 3-level config hierarchy: global → per-scope → per-memory-type
- 4 policy types: age-based, inactivity-based, combined, budget-based (top-N)
- Pinning mechanism: explicit pin + auto-protection rules (decisions, high-connectivity, chain heads)
- Gradual transition via continuous strength scoring, not cliff drops
- Safety: never compact chain HEADs, never compact entries younger than type's max_age

## Domain 6: Compaction Strategy
- Walk supersession chain, LLM-summarize preserving final state + key rationale
- New summary entry with compacted_from metadata, new embedding
- Originals archived (JSON preserved, embedding discarded)
- Warning: Knowledge Objects paper documents 60% knowledge loss from cascading compaction — be conservative

## Domain 7: HNSW Index Management
- hnsw_rs has NO deletion support — current orphan+rebuild approach is correct
- Blue-green rebuild: build new index in background, atomic swap under mutex
- Cold entries: brute-force cosine scan (< 2ms for 3K entries at 768-dim)
- Async promotion: return cold hit immediately, spawn background HNSW re-insertion
- PostgresStore: partial HNSW indexes with WHERE clause (pgvector 0.8+) — zero manual management
- Rebuild trigger: when orphan ratio > 20% of total entries

## Domain 8: Background Worker
- Hybrid: lazy evaluation at retrieval time + periodic batch worker (default 60min)
- In-process tokio::spawn, not separate binary
- Compaction rate-limited (max 10 per worker run)
- Circuit breaker: abort if >50% operations fail

## Domain 9: Working Memory Curation (RETRIEVAL LAYER — separate from tiers)
- Tier feeds into retrieval ranking but is not the same system
- Session/agent affinity boosts are retrieval-time concerns
- Hot/Warm/Cold modulates retrieval score: Hot=1.0, Warm=0.5, Cold=0.1
